definition: The pleasant, earthy smell produced when rain falls on dry soil, caused by oils released from the ground.
The realisation that each passerby has a life as vivid and complex as your own, with their own ambitions, worries, and routines.
The act of throwing someone or something out of a window, or the dismissal of someone from a position of authority.
A whispering or rustling sound, such as that made by wind through leaves or distant conversation.
The state of being infatuated or obsessed with another person, typically involuntary and characterised by a strong desire for reciprocation.
The warmth of the sun in winter; the feeling of warm sunlight on a cold day.
The sound of wind rustling through trees and their leaves.
The soul, creativity, or love put into something; doing something with passion and absolute devotion.
A deep emotional state of melancholic longing for something or someone that is absent, often carrying a repressed knowledge that what one longs for may never return.
Sunlight that filters through the leaves of trees, creating a dappled pattern of light and shadow on the ground.
The habit of acquiring books and letting them pile up without reading them.
An ache for distant places; a craving for travel and the longing to be somewhere you have never been.
Lasting for a very short time; transient. Often used to describe things whose beauty or significance lies in their fleeting nature.
Sweet-sounding; pleasant to hear, especially of a voice or piece of music that flows smoothly like honey.
Too great or extreme to be expressed or described in words; beyond what language can capture.
Extremely idealistic; unrealistic and impractical, inspired by noble but unattainable goals. From Don Quixote.
Soon passing out of sight, memory, or existence; quickly fading or disappearing like vapour.
Imposingly deep and full in sound; producing a rich, resonant tone that carries well.
Extremely delicate and light in a way that seems not of this world; heavenly or celestial in quality.
The occurrence of events by chance in a happy or beneficial way; a fortunate accident that leads to a pleasant discovery.
Pleasure derived from another person's misfortune; the guilty satisfaction felt when witnessing someone else's trouble.
A strong, innate desire to travel and explore the world; a restless longing for new experiences and distant places.
The strange wistfulness of used bookshops, filled with the ghosts of stories waiting to be discovered.
The luminous patterns and colours seen when you press on your closed eyelids, caused by stimulation of the retina.
The dark grey colour seen by the eye in perfect darkness, distinct from pure black. Literally 'intrinsic grey' in German.
A quality of cosiness and comfortable conviviality that engenders a feeling of contentment or well-being. A defining feature of Danish culture.
To go out in windy weather for a walk or other activity, especially to clear one's head. A Dutch concept with no direct English translation.
A manuscript or surface on which later writing has been superimposed on earlier writing that has been erased but remains faintly visible beneath.
An alignment of three celestial bodies, such as the sun, earth, and moon during an eclipse. Also used for any conjunction or pairing.
An excessive desire to stay in bed; an overwhelming urge to remain lying down rather than getting up and facing the day.
